{{神社 |名称 = 諏訪神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=文京区}} |所在地 = [[東京都]][[文京区]][[後楽]]2-18-18 |地図 = |祭神 = [[建御名方彦神別命|健御名方命]]、[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |社格 = |創建 = [[明徳]]元年（[[1390年]]）？ |本殿 = |別名 = 小石川諏訪神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 9月17日 |神事 = }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[文京区]]の[[神社]]。
== 歴史 == [[1390年]]（[[明徳]]元年）に創建されたといわれている。近くの[[北野神社 (文京区)|北野神社]]の別当をつとめていた梅本坊乗観が、出身地の[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]]の[[諏訪大社]]より分霊を勧請して創建された。そういう経緯もあり、現在も北野神社の兼務社となっている<ref name="神社">内海一紀 編『文京区神社誌』文京区神社総代会、1988年、39-40p,401-403p</ref>。
== 摂末社 == *稲荷神社（祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]）
== 交通アクセス == *[[後楽園駅]]より徒歩5分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *内海一紀 編『文京区神社誌』文京区神社総代会、1988年

| image = File:Suwa-jinja (Bunkyo-ku).jpg
| location = [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]], {{ill|Koraku|ja|後楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koraku|qq=|1=Kōraku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488350}} 2-18-18
| coordinates ={{Coord|35.706872|N|139.745468|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
| deity = {{ill|Q70537095|qid=Q70537095}}; {{ill|Yasakatome|qid=Q9150392}}
| founded = [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1)
| alternate_names = Koishikawa Suwa Shrine (小石川諏訪神社)
| festival = September 17
| location = {{ill|Bunkyo|qid=Q212713}}
}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|qq=|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in his birthplace, [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]]. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from [[:en:Kōrakuen Station|Korakuen Station]] Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988.
* {{cite book|title=[[:en:Edo meisho zue|Edo Meisho Zue]]|id={{NDLJP|1174144/299}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=巻之四 天権之部 諏訪神社|pages=588-589,591|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|qq=|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in his birthplace, [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]]. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from [[:en:Kōrakuen Station|Korakuen Station]] Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in his birthplace, [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]]. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from [[:en:Kōrakuen Station|Korakuen Station]] Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

== Notes ==

== References ==

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Bunkyō|Bunkyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in his birthplace, [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]]. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from [[:en:Kōrakuen Station|Korakuen Station]] Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in [[:en:1390|1390]] ([[:en:Meitoku|Meitoku]] 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in his birthplace, [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]], [[:en:Suwa District, Nagano|Suwa District]]. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from [[:en:Kōrakuen Station|Korakuen Station]] Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in his birthplace, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District}}. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyo|1=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.

== History ==
Suwa Shrine is believed to have been founded in {{ill|1390|en|1390|WD=Q6375|lt=1390|1=1390}} ({{ill|Meitoku|en|Meitoku|WD=Q1145032|lt=Meitoku|1=Meitoku}} 1){{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue|1927|p=591}}. It was established by Umehonbo Jokan, the bettō (chief priest) of the nearby {{ill|Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|ja|北野神社 (文京区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine (Bunkyo-ku)|1=Kitano Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277801}}, who enshrined a spirit from {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in his birthplace, {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}, {{ill|Suwa District, Nagano|en|Suwa District, Nagano|WD=Q1131473|lt=Suwa District|1=Suwa District, Nagano}}. Due to this connection, Suwa Shrine remains an auxiliary shrine of Kitano Shrine<ref name="shrine">Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988, pp. 39-40, 401-403</ref>.

== Sessha and Massha (Subordinate Shrines) ==
* Inari Shrine (Deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}})

== Access ==
*7-minute walk from {{ill|Kōrakuen Station|en|Kōrakuen Station|WD=Q1156432|lt=Korakuen Station|1=Kōrakuen Station}} Exit 1 ({{OSM経路図|start=35.70707,139.75158|goal=35.70686,139.74547|name=Route Guide}}).

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
* Kazunori Uchiumi (ed.), "Bunkyo-ku Jinja-shi," Bunkyo Ward Shrine General Assembly, 1988.
* {{cite book|title={{ill|Edo meisho zue|en|Edo meisho zue|WD=Q2704637|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/299}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Nagatoshi Saito|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=巻之四 天権之部 諏訪神社|pages=588-589,591|ref={{sfnref|Edo Meisho Zue|1927}}}}
